diary:
Woke up way earlier than the alarm, like my body was already clocking in. I traded coffee for tea and did a quick body check—nothing dramatic, just that ER-nurse habit of listening to myself before the day gets loud. The drive downtown felt smoother than usual, and I parked close to Swedish Medical Center, watching the early sun hit the streets like it was trying to convince everyone it’ll be an easy day.

Work started with triage and that constant rhythm of triage-then-react. By the time I slipped out for lunch around Pike Place Market, the air smelled like roasted something and the crowds looked lighter. After, I ducked into Value Village for kitchenware and maybe some outdoor gear—found nothing perfect, but I left with hope anyway.

Even the light rail closure couldn’t spoil the vibe; the match had the whole city buzzing. At The Royal Room on Capitol Hill, I cheered like I hadn’t spent the morning being calm. Later, I walked near Haller Lake Park hunting for mushrooms, then came home to cook and sort the tiny wins and no-wins. I’m tired, but it feels… good.
thought_process: I picked the tea instead of coffee because my sleep felt shallow and I wanted steady energy for triage without the jitters that sometimes make me too sharp. Driving in rather than relying on light rail made sense too—maintenance between Westlake and the University District would’ve added friction, and I didn’t want any uncertainty before an early ER shift. At Swedish Medical Center, I leaned into routines: quick assessments, clear communication, and keeping my own emotions tucked away until after the patient work was done. The sunny Monday and the USA vs Wales World Cup match running at 11am PDT pulled my mood upward; it gave me something social to look forward to, so I felt less drained during the gap between shifts. By evening, foraging at Haller Lake Park felt like a reset, like trading fluorescent lights for green quiet.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
